cards.title: EIS scope and planning role

citation.ieee_reference: [M10] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Electrochemical impedance test," pp. 40-43.
citation.title: Electrochemical impedance test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M10] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 40-43.

key_takeaways: The chapter treats EIS as an SOC- and SOH-resolved non-destructive diagnostic.
The measurement is framed at OCV rather than under dynamic load.
limitations: Equivalent-circuit interpretation is still model- and setup-dependent.
allowed_deviations: Life-stage checkpoint count may move only after review.

cards.title: EIS execution block

citation.ieee_reference: [M10] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Electrochemical impedance test," pp. 40-43.
citation.title: Electrochemical impedance test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M10] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 40-43.

key_takeaways: The execution loop is rest, spectrum, short rest, 20% SOC discharge step, then repeat until 0% SOC.
The handbook fixes the signal at 5 mV rms from 10 kHz to 10 mHz with 5-6 points per decade.
limitations: Lead and holder impedance remain practical error sources.
allowed_deviations: Do not change the SOC staircase or frequency window without explicit review.

cards.title: EIS outputs and interpretation frame

citation.ieee_reference: [M10] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Electrochemical impedance test," pp. 40-43.
citation.title: Electrochemical impedance test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M10] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 40-43.

key_takeaways: The chapter expects Nyquist-style spectra and resistance breakdowns such as Ra, Rb, Rt, and Rtot.
Equivalent-circuit fitting is an accepted quantitative output path.
limitations: Operator experience still influences the manual resistance extrapolation route.
allowed_deviations: If a richer model is fitted, preserve the simpler handbook-aligned resistance summary too.

cards.title: Thermal characterisation scope

citation.ieee_reference: [M11] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Thermal characterisation," pp. 38-39.
citation.title: Thermal characterisation
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M11] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 38-39.

key_takeaways: The method is about surface heat distribution during operation.
The intended use is thermal-model and cooling-system support.
limitations: The chapter does not define one fixed electrical profile.
allowed_deviations: Electrical profile choice may vary after review.

cards.title: Thermal characterisation execution block

citation.ieee_reference: [M11] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Thermal characterisation," pp. 38-39.
citation.title: Thermal characterisation
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M11] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 38-39.

key_takeaways: The execution has two stages: a pseudo-adiabatic insulated stage and a thermostated-metal-plate repeat.
Sensor coverage across the whole surface is explicit.
allowed_deviations: Do not remove the second cooled stage without an explicit review note.

cards.title: Thermal characterisation outputs

citation.ieee_reference: [M11] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Thermal characterisation," pp. 38-39.
citation.title: Thermal characterisation
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M11] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 38-39.

key_takeaways: The post-processing frame is a 0D thermal model and a qualitative split of Joule, reversible, and cooling terms.
limitations: The chapter explicitly describes the heat-source split as qualitative.
allowed_deviations: If a richer thermal model is added, preserve the simpler qualitative framing too.

cards.title: Thermal-impedance scope

citation.ieee_reference: [M12] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Thermal impedance test," pp. 44-46.
citation.title: Thermal impedance test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M12] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 44-46.

key_takeaways: The method aims at heat capacity, thermal conductivity, and heat exchange parameters.
The chapter positions it as a lower-cost, non-destructive alternative to calorimetry-style methods.
limitations: The final parameter set still depends on model fitting.
allowed_deviations: Current amplitude may be tailored after review.

cards.title: Thermal-impedance execution block

citation.ieee_reference: [M12] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Thermal impedance test," pp. 44-46.
citation.title: Thermal impedance test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M12] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 44-46.

key_takeaways: The execution keeps the cell at 25 C, uses sinusoidal self-heating, and scans several frequencies from 0.1 to 10 mHz.
The setup explicitly minimizes contact area and uses an IR thermopile.
limitations: The complete test can take roughly a day.
allowed_deviations: Do not change the frequency window without explicit review.

cards.title: Thermal-impedance outputs

citation.ieee_reference: [M12] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Thermal impedance test," pp. 44-46.
citation.title: Thermal impedance test
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M12] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 44-46.

key_takeaways: The expected outputs are a thermal-impedance spectrum and fitted thermal parameters from the finite-difference model.
limitations: Output quality still depends on the harmonics fit and model fidelity.
allowed_deviations: If a different fitting model is used, preserve the measured spectrum itself.

cards.title: Quasi-static swelling scope

citation.ieee_reference: [M13] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Quasi-static thermal tests," pp. 47-48.
citation.title: Quasi-static thermal tests
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M13] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 47-48.

key_takeaways: Despite the chapter title, the method is really about swelling across SOC and ageing rather than a generic thermal transient.
The stated use cases include pack design and SoC / SoH indication.
limitations: The chapter is concise and delegates most electrical detail to the paired capacity test.
allowed_deviations: Keep the link to the paired capacity-style cycle explicit.

cards.title: Quasi-static swelling execution block

citation.ieee_reference: [M13] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Quasi-static thermal tests," pp. 47-48.
citation.title: Quasi-static thermal tests
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M13] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 47-48.

key_takeaways: The measurement is synchronized with the battery tester and runs during a capacity-style cycle.
A digital dilatometer is the extra required hardware.
limitations: The chapter does not add extra timing detail beyond the paired capacity test.
allowed_deviations: Do not drop synchronization between electrical and swelling channels.

cards.title: Quasi-static swelling outputs

citation.ieee_reference: [M13] "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," v3.0, "Quasi-static thermal tests," pp. 47-48.
citation.title: Quasi-static thermal tests
citation.linked_reference_markdown: [M13] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding," White Paper, 2018, pp. 47-48.

key_takeaways: The outputs are swelling traces that can be split into reversible and irreversible components.
limitations: The chapter offers no special post-processing beyond the synchronized swelling trace.
allowed_deviations: If additional swelling metrics are added, preserve the raw synchronized trace.
